title: struthiopteris nodulosa
intro: Struthiopteris nodulosa, also known as the nodular leather fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Struthiopteris nodulosa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves in hot water, or used in tinctures and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
